label: U.S. Passport Card — Sex Designation
description: Update the sex designation on your U.S. passport card. This is tracked separately from the passport card name update because the two aspects have different legal statuses. The sex designation on U.S. passport cards is subject to the same federal policy restrictions as the passport book.
immutable_note: Federal executive orders issued in January 2025 suspended the X gender marker option for U.S. passport cards and require physician documentation for M/F changes. The same restrictions that apply to the passport book apply to the passport card. This policy is being challenged in court.
immutable_compassion_note: This is a policy situation, not a permanent legal reality. Advocacy organizations including Lambda Legal and the ACLU are actively challenging these orders. If this matters to you, the advocacy links below are the best source of current status.

process.summary: When federal policy permits: sex designation changes on a U.S. passport card are handled through the same DS-11 or DS-82 application as the book. The same restrictions that apply to the passport book apply here.

steps.description: Monitor current policy through LGBTQ+ legal advocacy organizations. The same policy restrictions that apply to the passport book apply to the passport card.

process.cost: No additional cost beyond the passport card application fee when available

gender_marker_change.status_note: The same federal executive orders that restricted passport book sex designations in January 2025 apply to passport cards. Only M and F are available. X is suspended. M/F changes require physician documentation rather than self-certification. Active litigation may alter this.

presence_level_content.some_guidance: This item is here so you know it exists and can track whether policy changes. You do not need to act on it now.

sources.label: National Center for Transgender Equality: Federal ID Updates
